input.description:
Use this tool when the user asks to:
Find conversations with a specific person, customer, or group
Locate ongoing or historical discussions about a topic
Identify active deal, project, or decision-related threads
Narrow the search space before retrieving full thread content
Do not use this tool when:
A specific message or thread ID is already known
Full message or thread content is immediately required (use retrieval tools instead)
Prefer structured search parameters. Use raw_query only when the user explicitly provides Gmail search syntax.
For thread messages, use get_messages to get the metadata of the messages.

properties.label: Keywords
properties.name: keywords
properties.hint: Free-text keywords to search for across subject and message content.

properties.label: From
properties.name: from
properties.hint: Filter messages sent by this email address.

properties.label: To
properties.name: to
properties.hint: Filter messages sent to this email address.

properties.label: Participants
properties.name: participants
properties.hint: Filter messages involving any of the specified email addresses.

properties.label: Labels
properties.name: labels
properties.hint: Restrict results to messages with specific labels.

properties.label: Has attachments
properties.name: has_attachments
properties.hint: When true, return only messages with attachments.

toggle_field.label: Has attachments
toggle_field.name: has_attachments
toggle_field.hint: When true, return only messages with attachments.

properties.label: Start time
properties.name: start_time
properties.hint: ISO 8601 timestamp specifying the start of the search window.

properties.label: End time
properties.name: end_time
properties.hint: ISO 8601 timestamp specifying the end of the search window.

properties.label: Limit
properties.name: limit
properties.hint: Maximum number of messages to return. This field defaults to 20. The maximum allowed value for this field is 50.

properties.label: Raw query
properties.name: raw_query
properties.hint: Advanced Gmail search query using native Gmail search syntax. Must not be combined with structured search parameters.

properties.label: Page Token
properties.name: pageToken
properties.hint: Page token to retrieve a specific page of results in the list.

properties.label: Include spam trash
properties.name: includeSpamTrash
properties.hint: Include threads from SPAM and TRASH in the results.

toggle_field.label: Include spam trash
toggle_field.name: includeSpamTrash
toggle_field.hint: Include threads from SPAM and TRASH in the results.

input.code:
require "time"

q = []

q << input['keywords'] if input['keywords']
q << "from:#{input['from']}" if input['from']
q << "to:#{input['to']}" if input['to']

Array(input['participants']).each do |e|
  q << "(from:#{e} OR to:#{e} OR cc:#{e} OR bcc:#{e})"
end

Array(input['labels']).each { |l| q << "label:#{l}" }

q << (input['has_attachments'] ? "has:attachment" : "-has:attachment") if input.key?('has_attachments')

q << "after:#{Time.iso8601(input['start_time']).to_i}" if input['start_time'].present?
q << "before:#{Time.iso8601(input['end_time']).to_i}" if input['end_time'].present?

gmail_query = q.join(" ")

{
  q: gmail_query
}
input.name: gmail query builder

input.code:
headers = input['headers']

subject = headers.find { |h| h[:name].to_s.downcase == 'subject' }&.dig(:value)
sender  = headers.find { |h| h[:name].to_s.downcase == 'from' }&.dig(:value)
cc = headers.find { |h| h[:name].to_s.downcase == 'cc' }&.dig(:value)
bcc = headers.find { |h| h[:name].to_s.downcase == 'bcc' }&.dig(:value)

{
  subject: subject,
  sender: sender,
  cc: cc,
  bcc: bcc
}
input.name: Get Subject & Sender
